title: minuartia peploides subsp. latifolia
intro: Minuartia peploides subsp. latifolia, also known as Alpine Sandwort, is a sub-species of the perennial herb Minuartia peploides, typically found in mountainous regions and characterized by its yellow flowers and narrow leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its diuretic and antiseptic qualities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. Alpine Sandwort has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving urinary tract infections, and supporting kidney function. In herbal medicine, the plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
